generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system for motion control in MABI MAX 100 robot. Constrained by safety cell with maximum angular velocity of 75°/s and working range [-90°, 90°]. Enables programmable motion profiles with real-time feedback loops for trajectory correction."
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloid gearbox integrated with motor for precise motion control in MABI MAX 100 robot. Positional feedback from dual encoders enables dynamic adjustment of actuation ranges to prevent collisions and operational limit exceedance."
]

instance SafetyInterlockSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Hardware-level interlock system enforcing ISO 10218-2 safety standards for industrial robots. Implements motion planning algorithms and network-based safety protocols to prevent hazardous conditions during robot operation."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular feedback for motion control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder positioned behind the cycloid gearbox providing precise feedback for gearbox dynamics monitoring and motion correction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input currents for operational health monitoring and power consumption analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance GearboxTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring gearbox operational temperatures to detect overheating conditions and potential mechanical degradation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance GearboxTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Reference model derived from UML class diagrams serving as the conforming model for the Digital Shadow implementation. Defines the structural and behavioral framework for the MABI MAX 100 physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD model representing component-connector architecture of the MABI MAX 100 robot system with hierarchical decomposition of motor, gearbox, and control components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SimulinkMotionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based behavioral model simulating motor dynamics, gearbox mechanics, and control system interactions for real-time performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance OperationalHealthIndicatorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Contextual abstraction model transforming raw sensor data into higher-level operational health indicators for targeted analysis and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationTool
]

instance AggregatedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces combining raw sensor readings from motor position, gearbox position, current, and temperature sensors for unified analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData, GearboxPositionData, MotorCurrentData, GearboxTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationTool
]

instance ProcessedOperationalMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed operational metrics derived from aggregated sensor data including performance indicators, anomaly detection flags, and health status assessments."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataStreamingProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation (e.g., Mosquitto) enabling lightweight, publish-subscribe data transmission from physical sensors to digital shadow components with real-time capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool supporting component-connector architecture definition and hierarchical decomposition of physical system components for digital twin implementation."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowReferenceModel, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram
]

instance SimulinkSimulationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink tool for detailed behavioral modeling of motor dynamics, gearbox mechanics, and control system interactions with real-time simulation capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkMotionModel
]

instance DataAggregationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tool implementing data preprocessing algorithms for aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data into higher-level operational health indicators and performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalHealthIndicatorModel
]

instance VisualizationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualization tool presenting aggregated data and operational metrics to stakeholders for system state observation and performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840Dsl : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller providing remote control interfaces, programmable motion profiles, and real-time feedback loops for trajectory correction in robot actuation."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlService
]

instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive monitoring service aggregating real-time sensor data (motor currents, gearbox temperatures, positional feedback) via one-way data flow to digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalMetricsInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data presentation service displaying aggregated operational metrics and system states through interactive dashboards for stakeholder observation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

instance ContextualAbstractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service transforming raw sensor data into higher-level operational health indicators for targeted analysis and decision support purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides HealthStatusInsight
]

instance MotionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing programmable motion profiles and real-time trajectory correction for robot actuation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational metrics including performance indicators, efficiency measurements, and resource utilization assessments derived from aggregated sensor data."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of current system states, component health statuses, and operational parameters for immediate stakeholder assessment."
]

instance HealthStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Higher-level operational health indicators derived from contextual abstraction of raw sensor data, enabling targeted analysis of system performance and potential anomalies."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment where MABI MAX 100 robot operates with digital twin providing monitoring and decision support capabilities."
    base:contains MABIMAX100PhysicalTwin, DigitalShadowSystem
]

instance MABIMAX100PhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical twin representing the MABI MAX 100 serial robot system with integrated sensing and actuation components for manufacturing automation tasks."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearboxMechanism, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor, GearboxTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital shadow system implementing passive monitoring services through aggregation and abstraction of contextual data traces from the physical twin."
    base:contains DigitalShadowReferenceModel, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram, SimulinkMotionModel, OperationalHealthIndicatorModel, AggregatedSensorData, ProcessedOperationalMetrics
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **fine-grained temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components, particularly through **data trace aggregation and abstraction** (as per the Digital Shadow Reference Model). While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) are not detailed, the system relies on **processing components** that consume input data traces and produce output data with **traceability** (e.g., transitive traceability of computed traces). The temporal granularity is implicitly governed by the **latency requirements** of the underlying simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods [28, 29, 30]) and general-purpose languages (C++, Java), which dictate the computational time scales for model execution. The **processing logic** (data calculation or simulation models) ensures real-time or near-real-time alignment between physical and digital states, though no specific latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are quantified in the provided context. The **purpose-driven design** of Digital Shadows (Section C) implies adaptive time scales based on use-case requirements, such as manufacturing process monitoring (e.g., injection molding [42]), where temporal alignment must align with operational rhythms (e.g., cycle times)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** are framed within a **reference model-based approach** (Section B), where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** serves as a foundational abstraction for concretization. The system follows a **processing-centric architecture**, where **Processing Components** (Section B) execute computational steps (data calculation or simulation models) to transform input data traces into output data. This aligns with **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML IBDs** or **AADL** (Section A), which define **component & connector perspectives** for system decomposition. The **conformance relation** (Section B) ensures that concrete realizations (e.g., UML class diagrams) adhere to domain-specific concepts, enabling **modular integration** of tools (e.g., Simulink, C++/Java). Resource management is implied through **traceability** (e.g., linking data traces to processing components and models), ensuring **fine-grained traceability** for debugging and validation. No explicit mention of **orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **resource allocation strategies** (e.g., load balancing) is provided, but the **purpose-driven design** (Section C) suggests adaptability to **use-case-specific coordination needs**, such as injection molding workflows [42]."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing distinct abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these phases is facilitated via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**, **ACME, AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language), MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**. These ADLs define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules, enabling seamless integration across heterogeneous tools (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java, or simulation tools**). Governance is implicitly addressed through **multi-perspective stakeholder integration**, ensuring alignment between domain-specific models (e.g., physics-based simulations for behavior) and engineering artifacts. The lifecycle governance approach emphasizes **interoperability** between modeling notations and tools, with explicit mention of **RFLP (Requirements-Functions-Logics-Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT** methodologies to manage complexity across phases. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are referenced, but the reliance on **MBSE and ADLs** implies adherence to iterative refinement processes common in systems engineering."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) abstraction layers**, including **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and physical layers**, to ensure model fidelity. **Validation and verification (V&V)** are conducted through **comparative analysis of simulated vs. measured data**, focusing on the **deviation scope** between computational models and real-world observations. While explicit **uncertainty quantification methods** or **error correction mechanisms** are not detailed, the framework implies **quality assurance processes** rooted in **abstraction consistency** across layers. **Static analysis** (e.g., logical consistency checks) and **dynamic validation** (e.g., real-time deviation monitoring) are likely employed, though their specifics remain implicit in the described methodology. The system prioritizes **data-driven fidelity** by aligning digital representations with physical asset states via **one-way data flows**, ensuring traceability and accuracy in the Digital Twin’s operational context.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The technical network infrastructure for this system is implicitly described through the **digital shadow reference model** [32], which emphasizes a **one-way data flow** from physical to digital without bidirectional control. Key technical aspects include:

- **Communication Protocols**: MQTT is referenced as a protocol for streaming sensor data from physical objects to the digital shadow [52]. No other protocols (e.g., OPC UA, WebSockets) are mentioned.
- **Network Topology**: The documents do not specify a topology (e.g., star, mesh), but the use of MQTT suggests a **pub-sub architecture** for distributed data dissemination.
- **Bandwidth and Latency**: Not explicitly detailed, though the system is designed for **real-time or near-real-time** monitoring (implied by the term 'digital shadow' and its purpose-driven aggregation).
- **Reliability and Redundancy**: No mechanisms for redundancy or failover are described. The system relies on the **passive nature of the digital shadow**, meaning it does not require active control or feedback loops.
- **Security Protocols**: Not mentioned in the provided documents. The focus is on data aggregation and abstraction, not network security.
- **Visualization**: The digital shadow’s data is visualized in a **dashboard**, though no specific tools (e.g., ThingsBoard) or frameworks (e.g., Angular) are referenced beyond general-purpose mentions in unrelated citations [53, 54].

The infrastructure is primarily defined by the **unidirectional data flow** and the use of MQTT for sensor data transmission, with no explicit details on network architecture beyond this.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The deployment infrastructure for this system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the following can be inferred from the context:

- **Hosting Platforms**: Not specified. The digital shadow is described as a **purpose-driven model** [56], implying it could be deployed in **cloud, edge, or on-premise** environments, but no concrete platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure) are mentioned.
- **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Not detailed. The system focuses on **data aggregation and abstraction**, which may require minimal computational resources, but no specific hardware or software constraints are provided.
- **Scalability Mechanisms**: Not mentioned. The digital shadow’s purpose-driven nature suggests scalability may depend on the aggregation tool’s capabilities (e.g., SysML or Simulink), but no orchestration or scaling strategies are described.
- **Deployment Models**: The system is described as an **executable digital shadow model** [56], implying a **model-driven deployment** approach, but no specific tools (e.g., Kubernetes) or deployment pipelines are referenced.
- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Not addressed. The digital shadow’s passive nature means reliability is tied to the **data source’s stability**, with no redundancy or fault-tolerance mechanisms explicitly outlined.

The deployment is implicitly tied to the **modeling and simulation tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) used for aggregation and abstraction, but no technical deployment specifics are provided.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **standardized interoperability frameworks**, including **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for semantic data exchange and **MQTT-based streaming protocols** for real-time sensor data transmission. The system adheres to **enterprise integration patterns**, leveraging **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., simulation tools, IoT devices) while maintaining **fine-grained traceability** via transitive associations between data traces and processing components. While explicit mention of **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin coordination** or **enterprise system dependencies** is limited, the **reference model’s conformance relations** enable seamless integration with other Digital Twin instances by enforcing consistent modeling conventions. The **passive nature of Digital Shadows** (as per Kritzinger et al.) implies reliance on external systems for active data processing, though the **Processing Components** within the Digital Twin system act as active intermediaries for derived insights."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The document references **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** as part of its Digital Twin implementation framework (Abstract), aligning with **industry standards for interoperability** in industrial systems. However, **specific ISO/IEC standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 19510, ISO/IEC 24744) are **not cited**. Key standardization aspects include:
- **AAS Compliance**: The Digital Twin adheres to **AAS-based data models** for semantic interoperability, enabling standardized information exchange between systems (e.g., manufacturing execution systems, ERP).
- **Interoperability Standards**: Leverages **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML IBDs**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc** to ensure consistency across heterogeneous tools (e.g., Simulink, general-purpose code).
- **Certification Requirements**: **Not explicitly mentioned**; no details on compliance frameworks (e.g., IEC 62424 for DTs) or certification bodies are provided.
- **Protocol Adoption**: Focuses on **model-based integration** (via ADLs) rather than low-level communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT). The **one-way data flow** (digital shadow) implies reliance on **standardized data formats** (e.g., JSON, XML) for aggregation but lacks protocol-specific details."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **physical safety mechanisms** through the **safety cell of the robot**, which restricts the **maximum working range** to predefined limits (e.g., **MABI Max-100 robotic arm**, [44]). However, **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., **IEC 62443**, **ISO 26262**), **encryption standards** (e.g., **AES-256**), **authentication systems** (e.g., **X.509 certificates**), or **OAuth 2.0** are **not mentioned** in the provided documents. The system does not detail **threat mitigation strategies**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **risk assessment approaches** beyond physical constraints. **Not in Document** for IT security, authentication, or safety-critical system standards."
]
